title: Stop containers in parallel fashion
body:
Prior, we were stopping containers serially.  So if a container had a default
timeout of 10 seconds and there were five containers being stopped, the operation
would take roughly 50 seconds.  If we stop these containers in parallel, the operation
should be roughly 10 seconds and change which is a significant speed up at scale.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body: @vrothberg can you PTAL at whats going on with the F28 podman in podman test? it is hitting some code you wrote?

> @vrothberg can you PTAL at whats going on with the F28 podman in podman test? it is hitting some code you wrote?

That comes from the `hack/dind` script. It looks like a hiccup on the F28 machine to me as mounting /tmp fails.
